description_long: This object is a heavy-duty pipe wrench, commonly used for gripping and turning pipes in urban maintenance and construction settings. The head is made of dark, robust metal with a toothed jaw and an adjustable mechanism, allowing it to clamp securely onto cylindrical objects. The handle is elongated, cylindrical, and appears to be made of wood, showing signs of wear and a slightly faded finish, which suggests frequent use. The metal head features a knurled adjustment nut and a prominent jaw, both of which are textured for grip. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a functional design prioritizing durability and leverage. The tool exhibits some surface scratches and discoloration, indicating it has been used in demanding environments.
description: A well-worn pipe wrench with a dark metal adjustable head and a long, weathered wooden handle, showing signs of frequent use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded end of the wooden handle and the broad, toothed jaw of the metal head.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the wrench, the adjustment nut, and the detailed jaw structure.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the end of the handle and the back of the adjustment mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the handle's texture and the profile of the wrench's head and jaw.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle with a slightly worn, smooth finish and visible grain texture
attribute_head_type: adjustable pipe wrench head with a toothed jaw and knurled adjustment nut, made of steel
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible scratches, discoloration, and wear on both the handle and metal head
